title: Hieracium tephranthelum
taxonomy: Hieracium tephranthelum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It is a species of hawkweed, which is a group of flowering plants in the tribe Cichorieae. Hieracium tephranthelum is a type of perennial plant, and its classification is further divided into the genus Hieracium and the subgenus Pilosella. Within the genus Hieracium, it is classified as a member of the section Hieracium, and it is characterized by its unique morphology and distribution. Hieracium tephranthelum is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, where it grows in rocky and grassy areas.

intro: Hieracium tephranthelum, also known as mountain hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of mountain hawkweed include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Mountain hawkweed is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
